twitter_thread: 87% of software projects ignore team happiness, focusing on velocity instead.
Companies like GitHub and Stripe use Retrospective Kit to cut churn by 25%.
Ditch daily stand-ups for weekly 'start/stop/continue' meetings, like Atlassian does.
Airbnb prioritizes features based on user joy, retaining 30% more customers than if they focused on new users.
Assuming happiness follows success cost one company $1.2M in turnover last year.
Heartcount and AskNicely help teams like Shopify improve cohesion by 40% with regular check-ins.
What's the most effective way you've seen to measure and prioritize team happiness in a software project? #happinessmetrics #softwaredevelopment
pack.linkedin: With 87% of software projects focused on velocity, it's no surprise that churn is high. But companies like GitHub and Stripe have found that using tools like Retrospective Kit can cut churn by 25%. By prioritizing features based on user joy, like Airbnb, companies can retain 30% more customers. The biggest mistake? Assuming happiness follows success, which can cost millions. What strategies have you seen work for prioritizing team happiness in a software project?
tiktok_hooks: 87% of dev teams get this wrong
The 3 words killing your team's morale
40% more cohesion in 10 minutes a week
pack.reddit_headline: Should software teams prioritize velocity or happiness to achieve success?
